comments.body: You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews. You can see your limits in the [Codex usage dashboard](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/settings/usage).

> [!IMPORTANT] > ## Review skipped > > Review was skipped due to path filters > >
> :no_entry: Files ignored due to path filters (2) > > * `.github/workflows/ci.yml` is excluded by `!**/*.yml` > * `.github/workflows/publish.yml` is excluded by `!**/*.yml` > >
> > CodeRabbit blocks several paths by default. You can override this behavior by explicitly including those paths in the path filters. For example, including `**/dist/**` will override the default block on the `dist` directory, by removing the pattern from both the lists. > >
> ⚙️ Run configuration > > **Configuration used**: Organization UI > > **Review profile**: CHILL > > **Plan**: Pro (Legacy) > > **Run ID**: `075acbfc-d314-4a1a-9f4a-cf42026cda77` > >
> > You can disable this status message by setting the `reviews.review_status` to `false` in the CodeRabbit configuration file. > > Use the checkbox below for a quick retry: > - [ ] 🔍 Trigger review ## Walkthrough GitHub Actions ワークフロー（CI/Publish 自動化）と beta チャネル対応のドキュメントを追加し、README にリリース手順を記載。eslint 設定を更新して未使用変数の検出ルールを拡充し、font-manager から未使用のインポートとヘルパー関数を削除した。 ## Changes |コホート / ファイル(s)|概要| |---|---| |**CI・Publish オートメーション & リリースドキュメント**
`CHANGELOG.md`, `README.md`|GitHub Actions ワークフロー（Push/PR 時の lint + test、manual publish 機能）と beta チャネル対応の説明を追加。npm dist-tag 使い分け（`latest` vs `beta`）、beta 命名規則、GA 昇格時の publish 方針、メンテナ向けリリース手順を記載。| |**ESLint 設定更新**
`eslint.config.js`|`no-unused-vars` ルール設定に `varsIgnorePattern` と `destructuredArrayIgnorePattern` を追加し、`^_` で始まる変数を統一的に無視する対象に拡大。| |**フォント管理の内部実装変更**
`lib/font-manager.js`|constants.js から `FONT_DEFAULTS` インポートを削除し、対応する未使用ヘルパー関数 `createFontImportBlock` を削除。| ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 2 (Simple) | ⏱️ ~10 minutes ## Poem > 🐰 **Actions と beta が整い > ドキュメント新たに光り > ESLint は清潔に > フォントの余分も削ぎ落とし > 本番への道は晴れやかなり** ✨
🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 3
✅ Passed checks (3 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :----------------: | :------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Title check | ✅ Passed | PRのタイトルは、GitHub ActionsのCI/Publish自動化とbetaチャネル追加というPRの主要な変更内容を正確に要約しており、開発者の視点から最も重要な変更を明確に表現している。 | | Description check | ✅ Passed | PR説明は、CI/Publish自動化、betaチャネル実装、ドキュメント更新、lintエラー解消など、変更セット内のすべての主要な部分と関連しており、充分な詳細度で関連性がある。 | | Docstring Coverage | ✅ Passed | Docstring coverage is 100.00% which is sufficient. The required threshold is 80.00%. |
✏️ Tip: You can configure your own custom pre-merge checks in the settings.

reviews.body:
**Actionable comments posted: 2**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@README.md`:
- Around line 51-52: The README currently describes two different GA promotion
flows (line ~51 says "re-publish X.Y.Z" while lines ~405-415 present a "publish
or dist-tag retag" choice); pick one canonical GA promotion procedure and make
the wording consistent across the document by updating the phrasing in both
places to the chosen approach (either always instruct "publish X.Y.Z" or always
instruct "retag via dist-tag / republish as needed"). Specifically, edit the
sentence at the earlier mention (currently "X.Y.Z を改めて publish") and the
paragraphs in the GA promotion section (lines ~405-415) so both use the exact
same action wording and steps, and ensure any examples or commands reflect that
chosen method.
- Around line 386-394: Add a precondition note to the "npm publish" workflow
documentation: state that the GitHub Actions job "Publish to npm" requires an
NPM authentication token (NPM_TOKEN) to be set in the repository/organization
Secrets and that the secret must be present and valid before manually running
the workflow (mention setting it under Settings -> Secrets and enabling Actions
access). Update the "安定版（latest）" publish steps to include a bullet before step
3 that instructs maintainers to ensure NPM_TOKEN is configured and, if relevant,
that the action has permission to read secrets and the token has publish rights
for the package scope.
```

</details>
